beats.name: hook
beats.evidence_note: Overview shows 8 tiles (auth-rewrite, codex-board, sse-stream, fix-flake, merge-conflict, old-experiment, docs-pass, tile-polish) with live streaming terminal content and a 'Needs input' pill on fix-flake. Caption: 'supermux — every tmux session at a glance'.

beats.name: hover-peek
beats.evidence_note: Hovered tile (tile-polish) is zoomed into the live-terminal peek with the spinner glyph and 'esc to interrupt' content visible. Caption: 'Hover any tile — it zooms in, live.'

beats.name: type-on-hover
beats.evidence_note: Same peek as beat 2 with the 'Typing → tile-polish — Esc to close' indicator visible at the bottom of the zoom card. Caption: 'Type while hovering — it lands in the real session.'

beats.name: focus-mode
beats.evidence_note: Focus mode for sse-stream — left session list, big live terminal showing cpu/mem/core graphs, 'Capturing input' badge top-right, keyboard chrome (Esc/Tab/Ctrl-C/Ctrl-U) at bottom. Caption: 'Click → smooth zoom into focus mode.'

beats.name: return-to-overview
beats.evidence_note: After clicking 'Back to overview', the overview re-renders. fix-flake tile is peeked showing the waiting prompt menu (1. Yes, extend / 2. No, poll cap / 3. Show me the).

beats.name: needs-input
beats.evidence_note: fix-flake tile carries the blue 'Needs input' pill and is peek-zoomed under the cursor. Caption: 'Sessions that need you pulse for attention.'

beats.name: cmd-k
beats.evidence_note: Cmd+K command palette open with filter text 'auth' typed and 'auth-rewrite' session result shown. Caption: '⌘K — jump anywhere.'

beats.name: density-and-sort
beats.evidence_note: Sort dropdown is open showing Smart / A–Z / Custom options. Density tier has been changed (denser 4-column layout). Caption: 'Density and sort — your overview, your way.'

beats.name: mobile-overview
beats.evidence_note: Phone bezel on left with the real mobile UI rendered via embedded iframe (390x844): header 'supermux'/'Overview', search bar, tile cards, '+' FAB, bottom tab bar (Overview/Board/Files/Scheduler/Settings). Marketing copy 'Same dashboard, on your phone.' on the right.

beats.name: mobile-focus-sheet
beats.evidence_note: Inside the phone bezel: tapping a tile navigated into the mobile focus sheet with the kbd accessory bar (Esc/Tab/^C/^U) docked at the bottom and a live terminal streaming 'Reading 1 file: …' content above it.

beats.name: outro
beats.evidence_note: Brand fade-out: large 'supermux' wordmark, tagline 'Self-hosted parallel AI coding sessions — overview, peek, focus, mobile.', github.com/sanderbz/supermux chip below.

notes: The viewport stayed at 1440x900 for the whole recording (Chromium recordVideo size is fixed at context-create). The mobile beats render an iframe pointed at '/?_token=…' inside a phone-bezel CSS overlay so the canvas remains full-bleed; the iframe carries a 390x844 viewport so the React app inside paints the real mobile layout (bottom tabs, FAB, accessory bar — all unmodified).

Sessions were seeded via the public HTTP API (POST /api/sessions then /start + /send) with shell commands chosen to trigger each status classifier: 'esc to interrupt'/'Reading N file'/'Running...' for Active (sse-stream, tile-polish, codex-board, auth-rewrite), 'Do you want to proceed' + '❯ N.' selector lines for Waiting (fix-flake), one explicit stop call for Stopped (old-experiment).

Captions were injected via page.evaluate into a fixed-position '#sm-caption' overlay; the outro overlay '#sm-outro-overlay' fades in over a soft brand-gradient backdrop. A fake cursor SVG follows page.mouse so the storyboard reads as a real person clicking.
